info.title: Rigbox API
info.description: Manage workspaces, apps, snapshots, tools, and more on the Rigbox platform.

get.summary: List all available catalog apps.

200.description: List of available catalog app items

get.description: Browses the catalog of installable apps and services. Each catalog entry includes a description, default configuration, and required resources.

get.summary: List all apps for the authenticated user.
get.description: Returns all apps across your workspaces. Use the `workspace_id` query parameter to filter by a specific workspace.

post.summary: Create a new app.
post.description: Creates a new app by mapping a port inside a workspace to a public subdomain on rigbox.dev. The app becomes accessible at `{name}.rigbox.dev`.

get.summary: Get an app by ID.
get.description: Returns details for a specific app including its public URL, port mapping, visibility setting, and current status.

delete.description: Deletes an app and removes its public route. The underlying service in the workspace is not affected.

patch.description: Updates an app's name, description, or other mutable configuration. The public URL changes if the name is updated.

get.summary:
Check if an app's URL is live by verifying actual content is served,
and probe the underlying service and port status.
get.description: Checks whether an app is live and its port is responding. Use this to verify an app is ready before showing its URL to users.

get.summary: List log sources for an app.
get.description: Lists available log sources for an app (e.g., stdout, stderr, or custom sources). Each source can be fetched independently.

post.summary: Create a log source for an app.
post.description: Registers a new log source for an app. Log sources define where log data is collected from inside the workspace.

delete.description: Removes a log source from an app. Historical logs from the source are no longer accessible.

patch.description: Updates a log source's configuration such as its file path or label.

get.summary: Fetch logs for an app.
get.description: Fetches historical logs for an app with pagination support. Use `lines` to control the number of log lines returned and `direction` for ordering.

get.summary: Stream app logs via Server-Sent Events.
get.description: Streams app logs in real-time using Server-Sent Events. Set `Accept: text/event-stream` and use `lines` and `interval_ms` query parameters.

get.summary: Get configurable parameters for an app.
get.description: Returns the configurable parameters for an app, as defined by its service spec. Parameters control runtime behavior.

patch.summary: Update configurable parameters for an app.
patch.description: Updates one or more configurable parameters for an app. Only parameters defined in the app's service spec can be set.

post.summary: Restart an app's service.
post.description: Restarts the backing service for an app. Useful after configuration changes or to recover from a stuck state.

post.summary: Start an app's service.
post.description: Starts the backing service for an app inside the workspace. The app must have a service spec or command configured.

post.summary: Stop an app's service.
post.description: Stops the backing service for an app without deleting the app or its route configuration.

get.summary: Get composite dashboard data.
get.description: Returns a combined dashboard view with workspace summaries, recent activity, and resource usage.

200.description: Dashboard data including workspaces, apps, limits, and AI credits

get.summary: Get feature flags resolved for the calling user.
get.description: Returns feature flags enabled for the authenticated user. Flags control access to preview features like access control and disk resize.

200.description: Feature flags for the calling user

post.summary: Create a workspace from a template.
post.description: Creates a workspace from a template in one call. This is the fastest way to get a running workspace — it provisions the VM, applies template defaults, and returns the workspace ready to start.

get.summary: Get all roadmap vote counts.
get.description: Returns the list of roadmap features and whether the authenticated user has voted for each one.

200.description: Roadmap vote counts and optional user votes

post.summary: Toggle your vote on a roadmap feature.

parameters.name: feature_key
parameters.description: Feature key to toggle vote on

post.description: Toggles your vote on a roadmap feature. Voting again removes your vote.

get.summary: List all service specs.
get.description: Lists all service specs for the authenticated user. Service specs define how apps run inside workspaces.

post.summary: Create a new service spec.
post.description: Creates a service spec defining an app's runtime configuration — port mappings, health checks, environment variables, and startup commands.

get.summary: Get a service spec by ID.
get.description: Returns a service spec by ID, including its full configuration.

put.description: Updates a service spec's configuration. Changes apply to apps that reference this spec on their next restart.

delete.description: Deletes a service spec. Apps that reference it will retain their current configuration but won't receive future updates.

get.summary: List all setup scripts.
get.description: Lists all setup scripts for the authenticated user. Setup scripts automate workspace initialization.

post.summary: Create a new setup script.
post.description: Creates a reusable setup script. Setup scripts run automatically when a workspace starts, and can install packages, configure services, or seed data.

get.summary: Get a setup script by ID.
get.description: Returns a setup script by ID, including its content and metadata.

put.description: Updates a setup script's content or metadata. Changes apply to workspaces that reference this script on their next start.

delete.description: Deletes a setup script. Workspaces that reference it will skip the script on next start.

get.summary: List all snapshots for the authenticated user.
get.description: Lists all snapshots across all workspaces for the authenticated user.

delete.description: Deletes a snapshot by its ID, regardless of which workspace it belongs to.

post.summary: Restore a workspace from a snapshot.
post.description: Restores a snapshot by ID to its original workspace. The workspace must be stopped.

get.summary: List SSH keys for the authenticated user.
get.description: Lists all SSH public keys registered to your account. These keys are synced to workspaces for SSH access.

post.summary:
Add an SSH key to your account.
The key is automatically synced to all running workspaces.
post.description: Registers a new SSH public key to your account. The key will be available for syncing to workspaces.

delete.summary:
Delete an SSH key from your account.
The key is automatically removed from all running workspaces.
delete.description: Removes an SSH key from your account. The key will no longer be synced to new workspaces.

get.summary: List available workspace templates.
get.description: Lists available workspace templates with their default configurations. This endpoint is public and does not require authentication.

get.summary: Get account-level AI defaults.
get.description: Returns the default AI provider settings that are applied to new workspaces when no explicit config is provided.

put.description: Updates default AI provider, model, and mode. New workspaces will inherit these settings unless overridden.

put.summary: Update AI mode for the authenticated user.
put.description: Switches between managed and BYOK AI mode. In managed mode, Rigbox injects provider API keys and tracks credit usage. In BYOK mode, you supply your own keys.

get.summary: Get AI usage history and workspace breakdown.
get.description: Returns AI usage history broken down by day and workspace. Includes token counts and cost estimates.

get.summary: Get AI credit balance for the authenticated user.
get.description: Returns the authenticated user's AI credit balance, total allocation, and current mode (managed or BYOK).

get.summary: Get effective account limits and current usage.
get.description: Returns account plan limits including maximum workspaces, apps, RAM, disk, and vCPU allocations, along with current usage.

get.summary: List available regions.
get.description: Lists available deployment regions. Use this to let users choose where their workspaces are provisioned.

get.summary: Get user settings.
get.description: Returns the authenticated user's settings and preferences, such as default region and editor configuration.

put.description: Updates user settings. Only provided fields are changed; omitted fields keep their current value.

get.summary: List all workspaces for the authenticated user.
get.description: Returns all workspaces owned by the authenticated user, including their current status, resource allocation, and metadata.

post.summary: Create a new workspace.
post.description: Provisions a new Firecracker micro-VM with the specified resources. The workspace starts in `provisioned` status — call the start endpoint to boot it.

get.summary: Get a workspace by ID.
get.description: Returns full details for a single workspace including status, IP address, resource allocation, environment variables, and template configuration.

delete.description: Permanently deletes a workspace and all associated data including apps, snapshots, and environment variables. This action is irreversible.

get.summary: Get the AI configuration for a workspace.
get.description: Returns the AI provider configuration for a specific workspace, including mode, provider, model, and whether a BYOK key is set.

put.description: Updates AI provider settings for a workspace. Accepts `mode`, `provider`, `api_key`, and `model`. Omitted fields keep their current value. Set a field to `null` to clear it.

post.summary:
Update environment variables for a workspace.
Changes take effect immediately if the workspace is running.
post.description: Sets or updates environment variables inside the workspace. Variables are persisted across restarts and available immediately in running workspaces.

post.summary: Detect or expose a port as a routable app.
post.description: Exposes a port from inside a workspace as a new app with a public URL. This is a shortcut that creates an app and sets up routing in one call.

post.summary: Install a catalog app into a workspace.
post.description: Installs a catalog app into a workspace. Installation runs as a background job — use the job-status endpoint to track progress.

get.summary: Poll the status of an async app install job.
get.description: Returns the status and progress of an asynchronous job such as an app installation.

get.summary: List ports with active listeners in a workspace.
get.description: Lists ports currently listening inside a workspace. Useful for discovering services that can be exposed as apps.

get.summary: Fetch structured logs from a workspace.
get.description: Retrieves workspace-level logs with optional pagination. Use the `lines` and `direction` query parameters to control output volume and ordering.

get.summary: Stream workspace logs via Server-Sent Events.
get.description: Streams workspace logs in real-time using Server-Sent Events. Set `Accept: text/event-stream` and use the `lines` and `interval_ms` parameters to control the stream.

get.summary: Get live resource metrics for a workspace.
get.description: Returns live CPU, memory, and disk usage metrics for a running workspace. Only available when the workspace status is `running`.

put.description: Resizes a workspace's RAM, disk, or vCPU allocation. The workspace must be stopped before resizing. Changes take effect on next start.

get.summary: List snapshots for a workspace.
get.description: Lists all snapshots for a specific workspace, including their creation time and size.

post.summary: Create a snapshot of a workspace.
post.description: Captures the current state of a workspace as a snapshot. The workspace must be stopped before creating a snapshot.

delete.description: Deletes a specific snapshot from a workspace. This frees the associated storage.

post.summary: Restore a workspace from a snapshot.
post.description: Restores a workspace to a previous snapshot state. This overwrites the current workspace disk. The workspace must be stopped.

get.summary: List SSH keys currently in the workspace.
get.description: Lists SSH keys currently deployed to a specific workspace's `authorized_keys` file.

post.summary: Sync all account SSH keys to the workspace.
post.description: Syncs your account's SSH keys into a workspace's `authorized_keys` file. Call this after adding or removing keys.

post.summary: Start a workspace.
post.description: Boots a workspace that is in `provisioned` or `stopped` status. The workspace transitions through `provisioning` to `running`.

post.summary: Stop a workspace.
post.description: Gracefully shuts down a running workspace. Disk state is preserved and the workspace can be started again later.

get.summary: List tasks for a workspace.
get.description: Returns tasks synced from the workspace's task manager, including their status and metadata.

put.summary: Sync tasks from td running inside a workspace VM.
put.description: Syncs task state from an external task manager into the workspace. Used by the sidecar to keep task data current.

get.summary: List available tools and their status for a workspace.

200.description: List of available workspace tools with status

get.description: Lists available tools and their current install/run status for a workspace.

post.summary: Install a tool asynchronously.
post.description: Installs a tool into a workspace. Installation runs asynchronously — use the install-status endpoint to poll for completion.

get.summary: Poll the status of an async tool install job.
get.description: Returns the current installation progress and status of a tool. Poll this endpoint after calling install.

post.summary: Launch a tool in the workspace.
post.description: Launches a tool that is installed in the workspace. The tool must be in `installed` or `stopped` status.

get.summary: Get the status of a specific tool.
get.description: Returns the current status of a specific tool in a workspace — whether it's installed, running, stopped, or not available.

post.summary: Stop a running tool.
post.description: Stops a running tool in the workspace. The tool can be relaunched later.

ApiErrorBody.description: JSON body returned for all errors.

configurable_params.description:
User-configurable parameters for this app.
Values are submitted at install time and resolved via `{PARAM_<KEY>}`
placeholders in install scripts, exec_start, and config files.

destructive_delete.description:
If true, deletion destroys user-modifiable files (e.g. scaffolded starter code).
Requires `?force_delete=true` and the UI renders extra confirmation
(type app name or check a warning box).

incompatible_templates.description:
Template IDs that already include this app (e.g. `["chat-app-starter"]`).
Installing on workspaces created from these templates is blocked.

min_disk_mb.description: Minimum disk space hint (MB). Informational only — not enforced.

min_ram_mb.description: Minimum RAM hint (MB). Informational only — not enforced.

port.description: Internal port the app listens on.

preview.description: Whether this item is in preview (feature-flagged).

live.description: Whether the app responded to an HTTP probe.

port_drift.description: True when the service is active but the expected port is not listening.

port_open.description: Whether the expected port is listening inside the workspace.

specs.description: The parameter schema (snapshotted at creation time).

values.description: Current values (from metadata.config). Sensitive values are masked.

DetectResult.description: Detection result for exposing ports — shows what was found before confirmation.

oneOf.description: Search for a file by name within $HOME.
Check if all listed binaries exist on PATH.

DiscoveryStrategy.description: How a tool discovers whether it's available in a workspace.

flags.description: Map of feature flag names to their resolved boolean values.

ImageInfo.description: Image descriptor — used by system/images endpoint.

credentials.description: Credentials generated at install time (only for apps that require login).

job_id.description: Job ID for async install — frontend should poll GET /workspaces/{id}/jobs/{job_id}.

live_installed.description: If workspace is running, whether live install was attempted.

app_status.description: Final app status after finalization (only set once job completes)

ListeningPort.description: A port detected as listening in a workspace.

LogLine.description: A single log line — used by workspace logs and app logs.

MessageResponse.description: Generic success envelope — used across all domains.

ParamSpec.description:
One configurable parameter declared by a template expected app or catalog item.

The `key` is used as the `{PARAM_<KEY>}` placeholder (upper-cased) in
install scripts, exec_start, and config_file JSON values.

description.description: Longer description / hint text shown below the field.

group.description: Visual group header for related params.

help_url.description: Help URL (e.g. docs for getting an API key).

order.description: Display order within the group (lower = higher).

param_type.description: Determines the UI element and basic validation.

required.description: If true, deploy is blocked when the value is empty.

sensitive.description: If true, render as password field and mask in API responses.

validation_pattern.description: Optional regex the submitted value must match.

ParamType.description: Data type for validation + UI element selection.

app_params.description:
User-supplied parameter values for template expected apps, keyed by app name.
E.g. `{ "gateway": { "ai_provider": "anthropic", "telegram_bot_token": "..." } }`

catalog_ids.description: Optional catalog app IDs to pre-install (Phase 3 composition).

expected_apps.description:
Expected apps created for this template (status=Installing).
Frontend should track these for install progress.

ServicePatchSpec.description: Specification for patching a systemd service with an EnvironmentFile.

env_file_path.description: Path to the env file to add (e.g. "/home/developer/.openclaw/.env").

remove_env_patterns.description: Environment= lines to remove if found (e.g. ["OPENCLAW_GATEWAY_TOKEN"]).

ServiceSpec.description:
Account-level service spec that runs a background process inside workspaces.
Working directory is always /home/developer.

environment_file.description: Optional path to an environment file for the service.

systemd_unit_preview.description: Preview of the systemd unit that would be generated

SetupScript.description:
Account-level setup script for reproducible workspace environments.
Scripts run once on first boot or on every start.

SnapshotView.description: Read-only projection of a workspace snapshot.

SshKeyEntry.description: An SSH key entry from a workspace's authorized_keys.

configurable_params.description:
User-configurable parameters for this expected app.
Values are submitted at deploy time via `app_params` in QuickDeployRequest.

available.description: Whether the tool is now available after install

needs_install.description:
True if the tool requires setup before it can analyze/launch.
Discovery may still work but full functionality needs install.

status_sys.description:
Lifecycle state reported by the tool's background process.
Values are tool-defined (e.g. "analyzing", "installing", "building").
None when idle or no background process is running.

unavailable_reason.description: Brief explanation of why the tool is unavailable.

coming_soon.description: If true, template is shown in UI but cannot be deployed yet.

env_file_copies.description: Copy .env to these additional paths at first boot.

has_tools.description: If true, this image ships the tools binary (ArchX, Virtual Browser, etc.).

managed_ai_config.description: If true, this template supports managed AI mode (proxy + credits).

service_patches.description: Systemd services to patch with EnvironmentFile at first boot.

verify_binaries.description: Binaries to verify exist at first boot.

install_hint.description:
User-facing install command when the tool needs setup.
Shown in the UI when the tool is unavailable or partially available.

port.description: Port the tool listens on inside the VM.

tags.description: Tags indicating supported languages, runtimes, etc.
